title: Routing and integration tests

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Routing and integration tests; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Testing Library; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Routing and integration tests

canonicalExplanation.summary: Route tests render with router context and assert URL-driven views, navigation, parameters, focus, and not-found behavior.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A test starts at /courses/c, activates Python, and asserts URL, heading, and focus context. Your goal is to create an integration test covering route navigation and focus-context changes. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Accessibility, errors and performance; Routing and application context; Asynchronous component tests; Route titles and focus management. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Route tests render with router context and assert URL-driven views, navigation, parameters, focus, and not-found behavior. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A mocked router can diverge from production, base paths matter, and direct-entry behavior needs integration coverage. This draft therefore rejects: Testing the component alone proves route configuration and browser history behavior.

includes: Verify navigation, URL state, direct entry and not-found behavior using a representative router setup.
Create an integration test covering route navigation and focus-context changes.
excludes: A mocked router can diverge from production, base paths matter, and direct-entry behavior needs integration coverage.

outcomes: Create an integration test covering route navigation and focus-context changes.

examples.title: Routing and integration tests: concrete case
examples.situation: A test starts at /courses/c, activates Python, and asserts URL, heading, and focus context.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A test starts at /courses/c, activates Python, and asserts URL, heading, and focus context.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Test components inside representative routing context and cover direct entry plus navigation history.
examples.result: The result follows because Test components inside representative routing context and cover direct entry plus navigation history.

examples.title: Routing and integration tests: boundary case
examples.situation: A mocked router can diverge from production, base paths matter, and direct-entry behavior needs integration coverage.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: A mocked router can diverge from production, base paths matter, and direct-entry behavior needs integration coverage.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Testing the component alone proves route configuration and browser history behavior.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Test components inside representative routing context and cover direct entry plus navigation history.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model Routing and integration tests, where inputs and state produce a UI description.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Routing and integration tests before using exact platform terms
limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.

recap.summary: You should now be able to create an integration test covering route navigation and focus-context changes. Keep this boundary in view: A mocked router can diverge from production, base paths matter, and direct-entry behavior needs integration coverage.

recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Intermediate single-page application”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
